read: TileLang can run the sparse dKV materialized-prob MFMA contribution body correctly on MI350 when block_k=64, block_d=32, and selected_window=64. This fixes the earlier K32 NaN/lowering branch and gives a real compact reduced-write kernel at small slot-aligned shape. It is not yet a target-shape speed candidate: full selected_window=512 still produces NaNs in the shared-staged contribution sink, the full active-group table does not lower once max_active_groups exceeds roughly 64, and the row-block workaround recomputes the MFMA body for every active-row block.

passing_gates.read: MFMA contribution body is finite and matches the PyTorch sink oracle at small shape.
The same contribution body remains correct at a clustered S128 route when selected_window is one K64 chunk.
Contribution production alone is finite but already slower than the retained Triton active-list compact sink, so the TileLang materialized-prob sink is not a speed path as-is.
The real compact row-block reducer is correct at small shape after moving from K32 to K64.
The row-block reducer stays correct, but it recomputes the contribution body per active-row block and is therefore much slower than the sink.

failed_or_rejected_gates.read: K32/D32 was the source of the earlier NaN branch.
Full selected-window accumulation remains poisoned in this materialized-prob layout.

failed_or_rejected_gates.error: loop_var_to_thread = d_i // 4 * 16 + k_i % 16 contains inner var k_i
failed_or_rejected_gates.read: Direct fragment reduction is not a valid TileLang lowering escape hatch for the sink.
The full active-group table lowers only at max_active_groups around 64; useful clustered shapes need a blocked active table or a different backend.

decision.next_kernel_step: Implement a blocked active-table TileLang/HIP/CK producer that keeps the K64 MFMA body but reduces only a bounded group block without recomputing the contribution body for every group block, or move this sparse segmented reduction to HIP/rocWMMA where LDS layout and warp segmented reduction can be controlled directly.
do_not_retry: one giant active-group table for clustered S128/S4096 routes
row-block route reducer as a performance candidate
